question_text: Fill in the blank: Effective communication is clear, honest, relevant, and _____.

Imagine that a restaurant is hosting a recruitment day to hire delivery drivers. The project manager creates a plan to organize stakeholder communication. The plan indicates the following:The team lead updates the core hiring team every day for two weeks prior to the event to report next steps so they each know their daily responsibilities.The core hiring team contacts the job candidates the Friday before the event to remind them of the time and location.The project manager contacts the venue and caterers the Monday before the event to confirm reservations. In the above scenario, what three key elements are missing from the communication plan?
Which of the following is a best practice you can use to help ensure that your communication plan is effective for your team? Select all that apply.

answers.answer_text: Share your plan with your manager but not your team.
Use formatting to highlight any key details.

question_text: How can you recognize individual differences in your communications? Select all that apply.

answers.answer_text: Be mindful of your own biases.
Include and respect diverse points of view.
Craft your communications based on what you assume about your audience’s backgrounds, identities, or experiences.
Use appropriate, professional, and neutral language.

question_text: Fill in the blank: In the communication plan you created, you scheduled _____ to keep your manager informed and get feedback.
In your communication plan, how often does your team communicate with Human Resources while developing the training plan?
In your communication plan, who is the Sender/Owner responsible for communicating the training schedules, locations, and other details to trainees?
In your communication plan, who coordinates with the Print Shop about printing the training manuals?
Which of the following should project managers identify before creating a communication plan? Select all that apply.
What should a project manager assume when writing a new email message for team members about project updates?

answers.answer_text: The message will be delivered to everyone in the company
No one will respond to your message
It’s safe to include sensitive or personal information
Not all of the recipients will read the message

question_text: What details does a communication plan include? Select all that apply.

As a project manager, you decide to hold video conferences with your core project team and stakeholders as part of your communication plan. What additional details should the communication plan include? Select all that apply.
You prepare a template for a weekly email for stakeholders with some key launch dates and announcements for the project. How can you ensure stakeholders can easily notice the information?

answers.answer_text: Create a follow-up email to be sent a day later with just the important information.
Schedule a follow-up meeting with the stakeholders to review the email.
Keep the key launch dates and announcements together for better clarity.
Highlight and bold the information the stakeholder should focus on.

question_text: As a project manager, how can you optimize and streamline project communications?

answers.answer_text: Create a static communication plan for the team
Be the only person who communicates with teams about the project
Only use one communication method with all teams
Send team members a survey asking about improvements to communication

question_text: Which of the following communication methods are most appropriate for core project team members? Select all that apply.

answers.answer_text: Formal presentations to explain project deliverables
Daily meetings to report on project progress
Quick virtual check-ins to answer questions

question_text: You create a project communication plan for a busy executive who needs high-level updates on the project. What type of communication would you create for this executive?

answers.answer_text: A newsletter email outlining key milestones and project progress
A email that outlines the roles of each team member and stakeholder
An email that contains a list of all the completed tasks
Daily meetings that the stakeholder is required to attend

question_text: What project management best practice includes documenting plans and making them available to stakeholders?

A project manager creates a centralized planning document. They create an overview sheet with the project description and communication expectations. What is another piece of information they can include in the overview sheet?

answers.answer_text: Instructions for how to use the spreadsheet
Goals for how many emails to send to stakeholders
Goals on how many daily check-ins for project
Instructions for how to use the RACI chart
